preamble: Lambdacism is the shift of a sound (often [r]) to [l]. It is a common liquid interchange, frequently occurring as a form of dissimilation or in specific dialectal developments.

examples.note: The suffix -alis becomes -aris if the root contains an 'l' (e.g., navalis vs solaris).
Historical shift of [r] to [l] in Eastern Indo-Aryan Prakrits.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
